ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An entomologist in agriculture studies the behavior, ecology, and management of insects that affect crops. They develop strategies to minimize crop damage and ensure food security.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Entomologists in agriculture work with farmers, researchers, and policymakers to understand the complex relationships between insects, crops, and the environment. They use this knowledge to develop integrated pest management (IPM) strategies, which combine physical, cultural, biological, and chemical controls to minimize harm to people, the environment, and beneficial insects.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Entomology or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in entomology can start by taking courses in biology, chemistry, and mathematics. They can also participate in insect-related projects and internships to gain hands-on experience.
target_degrees: B.Sc. in Agriculture or related field

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can bridge their skills by taking courses in entomology, ecology, and agriculture. They can also gain experience by working in agricultural research institutions or private companies.

future_builder.description: Experienced entomologists can advance their careers by taking on leadership roles, pursuing higher education, or specializing in a particular area of entomology.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: At least 2 years of experience as a Junior Entomologist or Research Assistant
advanced_skills_to_learn: Statistical analysis using R or Python

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into entomology by acquiring relevant skills and experience. They can start by taking online courses, attending workshops, and participating in insect-related projects.
survival_skills_needed: Basic knowledge of biology and chemistry

progression.senior_level: Lead Entomologist or Research Scientist (5+ yrs)

roadmap.title: Ecological Principles and Pest Management
resources_keywords: University courses or online resources (e.g., Coursera)

roadmap.title: Capstone Project and Portfolio Development
topics: Development of a portfolio showcasing skills and experience
resources_keywords: GitHub or other version control platforms
Kaggle or other data science platforms
